factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with restructuring expenses incurred but within expectations.

evidence.text: We incurred pre-tax restructuring and impairment expenses of $6.7 million in the first quarter of 2018, primarily related to employee severance and compensation-related costs, building lease exit costs and the impairment of assets.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable with restructuring expenses within expectations.

factors.summary: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, with costs on track.

evidence.text: We are providing non-GAAP measures (adjusted earnings, adjusted earnings per share, and adjusted segment earnings) that exclude restructuring and impairment expenses. Reconciliations to measures on a GAAP basis are provided later in this section.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, with costs on track.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through restructuring and cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: The majority of the consolidation of operations occurred in the second quarter of 2018. As a result of the relocation of production, we incurred pre-tax restructuring and impairment expenses of $6.7 million in the first quarter of 2018, primarily related to employee severance and compensation-related costs, building lease exits costs and the impairment of assets.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving through restructuring and cost reduction initiatives.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding in key markets and improving operational efficiency.

evidence.text: We expect our full year segment margin to be equal to or slightly down compared to full year 2017 segment margin of 13.4 percent. We expect our local currency sales in China to grow approximately six percent in the second half of 2018, slightly higher than our China local currency sales growth in the first half of 2018.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategic direction focusing on expanding in key markets and improving efficiency.

factors.summary: The company's revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products and geographies, particularly in North America and India, which is expected to contribute to margin improvements and sales growth.

evidence.text: We expect sales in India to grow over 40 percent in 2018 from approximately $26 million in 2017. Combining all of these factors, we expect our consolidated sales to grow between 9.5 percent and 10 percent in 2018.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Higher sales of water heaters and boilers contributed to higher segment margins in North America and Rest of World segments.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifting towards higher-margin products and geographies, contributing to margin improvements and growth.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, successfully implementing price increases and achieving favorable impacts on its gross profit margins, despite facing higher input costs.

evidence.text: In the first quarter of 2018, we announced a price increase, which averaged ten percent, on our U.S. wholesale water heaters due to steel, freight and other cost inflation. The price increase was effective in early June. Our sales of boilers grew 13 percent in 2017, and we expect ten percent sales growth in 2018, driven by the continuing U.S. industry transition to higher efficiency products and our introduction of new products.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: The favorable impact of higher sales of water heaters and boilers, as well as pricing actions in the U.S., were offset by higher steel and other input costs in the second quarter of 2018.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power with successful price increases and positive impact on margins despite higher input costs.

factors.summary: The order backlog is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue growth, indicating strong demand. However, this growth is accompanied by expected short-term losses due to transition costs.

evidence.text: We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by more than 75 percent in 2018, compared to 2017, primarily due to our new business at Lowe’s and a full year of sales of Hague products.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Sales in the North America segment were $534 million in the second quarter of 2018 or $63 million higher than sales of $471 million in the second quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

evidence.text: Sales for the first six months of 2018 were $1,036 million or $78 million higher than sales of $958 million in the same period last year.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing backlog with new orders outpacing revenue growth, indicating strong demand, despite short-term transition costs.

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in North America and India, with strong growth in sales volumes and pricing actions contributing to higher sales.

evidence.text: We project our sales in the U.S. will grow in 2018 compared to 2017 due to higher residential water heater and boiler volumes resulting from expected industry-wide new construction growth as well as announced pricing actions and expansion of replacement demand. We expect the North America residential water heater industry to have four to 4.5 percent unit growth in 2018. We expect sales in India to grow over 40 percent in 2018 from approximately $26 million in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Sales for the second quarter of 2018 were $833 million or approximately 13 percent higher than sales of $738 million in the second quarter of 2017. Sales in the first six months of 2018 were $1,621 million or approximately ten percent higher than $1,478 million in the same period last year.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating demand in key markets with strong growth in sales volumes and pricing actions.

We expect the North America residential water heater industry to have four to 4.5 percent unit growth in 2018.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly driven by increases in steel and freight expenses. While the company has implemented pricing actions to offset these cost increases, such as a 10% price hike on U.S. wholesale water heaters, these measures only partially mitigate the higher input costs.

evidence.text: In the first quarter of 2018, we announced a price increase, which averaged ten percent, on our U.S. wholesale water heaters due to steel, freight and other cost inflation. The price increase was effective in early June.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: The favorable impact of higher sales of water heaters and boilers, as well as pricing actions in the U.S., were offset by higher steel and other input costs in the second quarter of 2018.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While input costs are rising and affecting margins, pricing actions partially offset these increases.

factors.summary: The end markets, particularly North America and India, are showing strong growth. North America and Rest of World segments are growing rapidly, driven by increased sales in key products like water heaters and boilers, especially in China.

evidence.text: We expect sales in India to grow over 40 percent in 2018 from approximately $26 million in 2017. We expect sales in China to grow in 2018 at a rate of approximately five percent in local currency, and over eight percent in U.S. dollar terms.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Sales in the North America segment were $534 million in the second quarter of 2018 or $63 million higher than sales of $471 million in the second quarter of 2017. Sales in the Rest of World segment were $308 million in the second quarter of 2018 or $35 million higher than sales of $273 million in the second quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in end markets, particularly in North America and India, is driving revenue expansion.

factors.summary: Management has maintained and raised forward guidance for revenue growth in 2018, initially expecting between 9.5 to 10 percent and later increasing the midpoint of EPS and adjusted EPS guidance due to strong demand in the North America segment.

evidence.text: Combining all of these factors, we expect our consolidated sales to grow between 9.5 percent and 10 percent in 2018.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: We expect consolidated sales to grow between 9.5 and ten percent in 2018. With the North America segment bolstered by expected continued demand in all parts of the business, we increased the midpoint of our EPS and adjusted EPS guidance for 2018.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management has raised forward guidance, indicating confidence in future revenue growth.

factors.summary: The company faces mixed near-term risks, including both financial impacts from currency fluctuations and operational challenges such as high inventory levels and potential security breaches.

evidence.text: The impact of a weaker China currency compared to our April forecast reduced our guidance for full year sales by $22 million. We experienced lower than expected air purifier and e-commerce sales in the first half of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: We also expect losses in India to decline from a $7.5 million loss in 2017 to a $5 million loss in full year 2018. Forward-looking statements included in this filing are made only as of the date of this filing, and we are under no obligation to update these statements to reflect subsequent events or circumstances.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces near-term risks from currency fluctuations and operational challenges.

factors.summary: Gross margins are stable or improving due to pricing actions and higher-margin product sales, but operating margins are under pressure from higher input and labor costs.

evidence.text: We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by more than 75 percent in 2018, compared to 2017, primarily due to our new business at Lowe’s and a full year of sales of Hague products. The transition to the A. O. Smith brand at Lowe’s will take place beginning in August 2018. We expect sales of approximately $15 million and a loss of between $1 million to $2 million due to start-up and other transition costs from the new business in 2018.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Gross profit margin in the second quarter of 2018 of 40.9 percent was lower than the gross profit margin of 41.2 percent in the second quarter of 2017. The favorable impact of higher sales of water heaters and boilers, as well as pricing actions in the U.S., were offset by higher steel and other input costs in the second quarter of 2018.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are stable or improving, but operating margins are under pressure from higher costs.

factors.summary: Management confidence is evident through strategic actions such as new product launches, pricing increases, and partnerships, as well as financial moves like increased share buybacks and maintained dividend payments.

evidence.text: In April 2018, we announced we were named the primary supplier of water treatment products to all Lowe’s stores in the U.S. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by more than 75 percent in 2018, compared to 2017, primarily due to our new business at Lowe’s and a full year of sales of Hague products.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $135 million on stock repurchases through our Rule 10b5-1 automatic trading plan in 2018. In addition, we may opportunistically buy shares in the open market. On July 9, 2018, our Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.18 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management confidence is evident through strategic actions and financial moves.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to commodity prices, particularly steel and freight costs, as well as foreign exchange rates. Additionally, interest rates are a notable factor, though their impact was somewhat mitigated by lower debt levels in 2018.

evidence.text: In the first quarter of 2018, we announced a price increase, which averaged ten percent, on our U.S. wholesale water heaters due to steel, freight and other cost inflation. The impact of a weaker China currency compared to our April forecast reduced our guidance for full year sales by $22 million.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Higher interest rates in 2018 were offset by lower debt levels, primarily due to the repatriation of approximately $240 million of cash from outside of the U.S., which was used to pay down floating rate debt. Our effective income tax rates for the second quarter and first six months of 2018 were 21.6 percent and 21.4 percent, respectively.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant sensitivity to commodity prices and foreign exchange rates, which have negatively impacted guidance.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches, such as the transition to the A. O. Smith brand at Lowe’s, and strategic partnerships like the new business at Lowe’s. Additionally, there are near-term catalysts such as cost savings milestones from restructuring initiatives, though these come with initial costs and margin pressures.

evidence.text: In April 2018, we announced we were named the primary supplier of water treatment products to all Lowe’s stores in the U.S. The transition to the A. O. Smith brand at Lowe’s will take place beginning in August 2018.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / OVERVIEW

evidence.text: We expect full year adjusted segment margin to be between 22.25 and 22.5 percent in 2018, negatively impacted by start-up and transition costs related to our new water treatment product business at Lowe’s stores in the U.S. The majority of the consolidation of operations occurred in the second quarter of 2018.
evidence.subsection:
SECOND QUARTER AND
FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2018 COMPARED TO 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has new product launches and strategic partnerships, which are positive catalysts despite some short-term margin pressures.
